name: The Antiquarian Fence
concept: You buy what fell off a cart and sell it to people who ask no questions, and along the way you have learned more about this city's forgotten history than any scholar — because the past keeps turning up stolen. You know what old things are worth, and what they will do, and which of them should never have been dug up at all.
duty: Every treasure has a buyer, and every buyer has a leash. Your web of collectors keeps you rich and keeps you owned; the trick is selling the relic without becoming one.

look.label: Bearing
options: genial and shrewd
unhurried and dry
nervous around the wares
smooth as a closed deal

look.label: Dress
options: shabby-genteel
one fine piece amid the plain
a scholar's ink-stained coat
deliberately modest, to hide the money

look.label: Shop
options: a cluttered curiosity-stall
a back room of locked cases
no shop at all, only contacts
a respectable front over a crooked cellar

look.label: Tell
options: a jeweler's loupe always to hand
ink-stained fingers
a way of holding old things too gently
eyes that price everything you own

gear.label: Trade
options: a loupe, scales, and test-acids
a ledger of buyers and their appetites
a strongbox with a clever lock

gear.label: Cover
options: a respectable shop-front
a scholar's letters of introduction
a hidden vault no one knows of

gear.label: Piece
options: an unsold relic you're half-afraid of
a forgery good enough to fool a temple
a debt owed by a very important collector

moves.name: What It's Worth
moves.trigger: When you appraise a thing and set about turning it into coin or leverage.

results.success: You know its true worth and find a buyer or a use for it.
results.failure: You misprice it, or the only buyer is one you'd rather not deal with.
advantage: Hold 1 Connections (spend: name a collector here who wants exactly this / a buyer who owes you a favor / a discreet appraiser or forger on call / a rumor of where more of it can be found).
You know the thing's history — and its secret, dangerous or profitable.
threat: The sale leaves a thread the Guild or the law can follow.
The buyer wants more than coin — a favor, a silence, a further job.
results.triumph: You find the one buyer who will pay a fortune and ask nothing.
results.despair: The piece is hotter than you knew, and its true owner is coming for it, and you.

hold.name: Connections
spend: Name a collector here who wants exactly this.
Call in a buyer who already owes you a favor.
Produce a discreet appraiser, forger, or go-between on short notice.
Know a rumor of where more of this kind of thing can be found.

moves.name: The Collectors' Web
moves.trigger: When you draw on your network of collectors, forgers, and black-market buyers (Invoke Patron, core.md):
moves.text: The web provides — credit, discretion, a market for anything — and adds 1 Debt. Your patrons are wealthy, patient, and possessive; disappoint a collector, or sell them a fake they detect, and the debt is collected in ways coin cannot cover.

moves.name: Old Lore
moves.trigger: When you dig into the history, provenance, or hidden nature of an old or arcane thing.

results.success: Ask the GM one: what is it, truly? what does it do? who made it, and who wants it now?
results.failure: The lore is tangled or false, and acting on it will mislead you.
advantage: You learn how to use it, or how to make it safe.
threat: Your questions reach the wrong scholarly ear.
Knowing what it is, you now cannot un-know the danger.
results.triumph: You understand it completely, worth and peril alike.
results.despair: You've learned the thing's secret, and it has learned you know.

moves.name: Drive the Bargain
moves.trigger: When you haggle, flatter, or maneuver toward the deal you want.

results.success: You close a deal you can live with.
results.failure: No deal, or one whose price you'll come to regret.
advantage: A favor or morsel of news thrown in.
A standing arrangement for the future.
results.triumph: You get everything and their gratitude besides.
results.despair: The deal was the trap, and you've stepped in it.

moves.name: Spot the Fake
moves.trigger: When you examine a thing, a document, or a story for whether it's genuine.

results.success: You know true from false, and how you know.
results.failure: You're taken in, or you cry fake on the genuine article.
advantage: You spot who made the fake and why.
You could make a better one yourself.
threat: The forger learns you're onto them.
results.triumph: You see every seam; nothing gets past you.
results.despair: You stake something real on a judgment that's dead wrong.

moves.name: Discreet Word
moves.trigger: When you need a name, a rumor, or a quiet introduction in the world of buyers and thieves:
moves.text: Take a boost die to reach the right person through your web. The GM says what small price, gossip, or obligation rides along with the favor.

bonds: ____ brings me the best of what they lift, and I give them the honest price, mostly.
____ once sold me a fake, and I have been waiting a long time to repay that.
A collector I fear has taken an interest in ____, and I have not warned them.
____ trusts me to turn our scores into a future; I have not told them how deep my Debts run.
I am keeping a relic safe for ____, and it frightens me more each night.
communityTie: The Black Cat's scores only pay because you can move them; ____ handles the goods no one else will touch.
Your collector-patrons would pay to know the band's business; ____ is the confidence you'll never sell.
Old Sabra fronts you coin against the next sale; you both know exactly how much the den depends on it.

tracks.name: Bond
tracks.note: Standing with your web of collectors (core.md).

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 a powerful collector makes a major demand, then resets to 3.

tracks.name: Heat
tracks.note: Attention from the Guild, the law, and robbed owners (core.md).

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Connections by 1.
Cultivate a great buyer: name a wealthy collector who will take almost anything from you.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Buy down your Debt by 1, permanently, with a legendary sale.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Go legitimate, or go to ground: cut loose from the collectors' web, resolving the fallout in play.
Open the great emporium: found a Bastion (a market and vault) that makes the band a power in stolen history.
